rag_rules: Place AGREE assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes when they refer to visible ports and data types.
Use visible identifiers from the AADL model to define assumptions and guarantees; avoid implementation-specific references in type contracts.
generic_rules: Declare helper variables with explicit types to match the expected data types in AGREE expressions.
Generate range assumptions using named constants or visible feature names when bounds are specified.
Write guarantees that relate visible outputs to visible inputs without relying on internal subcomponent paths.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annex blocks must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` syntax.
Assumptions and guarantees must reference valid identifiers present in the AADL model scope.
anti_patterns: Avoid placing implementation-level bindings in component type annexes unless explicitly required.
Do not encode AADL properties like timing or unit tokens as AGREE variables.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property associations and their mapping to AGREE expressions
Implementation-level subcomponent port usage in type contracts
